description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: city in the state of Texas, United States
description_wiki: Dallas is a city in the U.S. state of Texas.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

description: Evangelical Christian church and charitable organisation
1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Long Beach is a coastal city in southeastern Los Angeles County, California, United States.

description: 1919 Tennessee-class battleship
description_wiki: USS California, hull number BB-44, was the second of two Tennessee-class battleships built for the United States Navy between her keel laying in October 1916 and her commissioning in August 1921.

description: harbor on the island of Oahu, Hawaii, USA
description_wiki: Pearl Harbor is a lagoon harbor on the island of Oʻahu, Hawaii, United States, west of Honolulu.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: 1914 Nevada-class battleship
description_wiki: USS Oklahoma (BB-37) was a Nevada-class battleship built by the New York Shipbuilding Corporation for the United States Navy, notable for being the first American class of oil-burning dreadnoughts.

description: 1914 Nevada-class battleship
description_wiki: USS Nevada (BB-36), the third United States Navy ship to be named after the 36th state, was the lead ship of the two Nevada-class battleships.

description: 1921 Colorado-class battleship
description_wiki: USS West Virginia (hull number BB-48) was the fourth dreadnought battleship of the Colorado class, though because Washington was cancelled, she was the third and final member of the class to be completed.

description: 1920 Colorado-class battleship
description_wiki: USS Maryland, hull number BB-46, also known as "Old Mary" or "Fighting Mary" to her crewmates, was a Colorado-class battleship.

description: 1919 Tennessee-class battleship
description_wiki: USS Tennessee, hull number BB-43, was the lead ship of the Tennessee class of dreadnought battleships built for the United States Navy in the 1910s.

description: 1915 Pennsylvania-class battleship
naval branch of the Empire of Japan
description_wiki:
The Imperial Japanese Navy (IJN) was the navy of the Empire of Japan from 1868 to 1945, when it was dissolved following Japan's surrender in World War II. The Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF) was formed between 1952 and 1954 after the dissolution of the IJN.
The IJN was the third largest navy in the world by 1920, behind the Royal Navy and the United States Navy (USN).

description: archipelagic country in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: The Philippines, officially the Republic of the Philippines, is an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia.

description: United States Army general in WWI, WWII and Korea (1880–1964)
description_wiki: Douglas MacArthur (26 January 1880 – 5 April 1964) was an American general who served as a top commander during World War II and the Korean War, achieving the rank of General of the Army.

description: Marginal sea in the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: The South China Sea is a marginal sea of the Western Pacific Ocean.

description: 1915 Pennsylvania-class battleship
description_wiki: USS Pennsylvania, hull number BB-38, was the lead ship of the Pennsylvania class of super-dreadnought battleships built for the United States Navy in the 1910s.
